tweets.topic: How safe are you from fraud during Amazon and Flipkart's Republic Day sales, and can cyber insurance...
tweets.tweet: Amazon and Flipkart's Republic Day sales expose millions to fraud, with cyber insurance costing ₹5,000-₹15,000 annually for basic coverage. You're basically paying extra to protect yourself from the same platforms promising you deals — welcome to modern Indian e-commerce.

tweets.topic: Internet subscribers in India grew 1.1 pc in October-December 2025 quarter: TRAI
tweets.tweet: Internet subscribers in India grew 1.1% in the October-December 2025 quarter according to TRAI data. Basically, we're adding users at a snail's pace when half the country still can't afford decent broadband speeds. #IndianTelecom #DigitalIndia

tweets.topic: Starlink, Jio, Airtel face further delay in satellite internet rollout amid TRAI–DoT standoff
tweets.tweet: Starlink, Jio, and Airtel's satellite internet plans hit another roadblock as TRAI and DoT remain locked in disagreement over regulations. Your dream of high-speed internet from the sky keeps getting postponed while bureaucrats argue — classic India tech launch story.

tweets.topic: To Counter Online Scams and Fraud, Address Consumer Vulnerabilities
tweets.tweet: Government launches new cybersecurity initiative to combat online fraud targeting Indian consumers with stricter guidelines for digital payments. Finally someone realized scammers are winning — now let's see if our passwords survive the bureaucratic paperwork.

tweets.topic: Internet subscribers in India grew 1.1% in October-December 2025 quarter: TRAI
tweets.tweet: Internet subscribers in India grew 1.1% in October-December 2025, reaching a new milestone according to TRAI data. Sounds modest until you realize millions more Indians are finally getting online — whether they can afford the data plans is another story.

tweets.topic: Delhi Delivery Agent Defrauds Amazon, Flipkart, Used To Return Old Items
tweets.tweet: Delhi delivery agent arrested for returning old items to Amazon and Flipkart instead of genuine products, defrauding both companies of lakhs. So your "new" phone might actually be someone's refurbished reject — thanks to the guy who was supposed to deliver it safely.

tweets.topic: India’s Telecom Subscribers Reach 1.31 Billion; Jio, Airtel Dominate
tweets.tweet: India's telecom subscriber base surged to 1.31 billion, with Jio and Airtel controlling the majority market share. Meanwhile, your monthly bills keep climbing because competition is apparently just a word in the dictionary. #IndianTelecom #MobileData

tweets.topic: Supply-chain scam: Ahmedabad police dismantle gadget-swapping interstate syndicate
tweets.tweet: Ahmedabad police busted an interstate gadget-swapping syndicate that was duping customers with fake devices across states. So your "new" phone might actually be someone's refurbished return — trust nobody, check serial numbers always. #CyberCrime #IndianTech

tweets.topic: Redmi 15A 5G with 6300mAh battery launched in India: Check price, specs
tweets.tweet: Redmi 15A 5G launched in India with a massive 6300mAh battery for budget hunters. Finally, affordable 5G that doesn't ghost you by noon — though your data bill might. #Redmi #5GTech

tweets.topic: Govt plans new smartphone export incentives in a boost for Apple, Samsung
tweets.tweet: Government announces fresh smartphone export incentives targeting Apple and Samsung manufacturing in India. Great news for factory workers, but consumers will still pay premium prices for the same devices. #MakeinIndia #TechPolicy
